meaning: adj.雉堞状的,在墙上打枪眼[炮眼]
examples: The outer parapet is crenelated with merlons almost 2 m high.
The paladin retreated up the beast's crenelated back as more creatures poured out of their lairs.
Presenting all these features, the facade on Boulevard is such an urban sculpture so immaculate and crenelated, enlivened by rippling arabesques.
Overlooked by a crenelated section of the Rocky Mountains known as the Ramparts, the scenery is spectacular, photogenic and remote (the nearest road is 18km away).
Built in 1999, the imposing edifice boasts three bedrooms, an indoor pool, a wine cellar and a heated driveway--not to mention two crenelated towers, drawbridge and central vac.
